---
title: "aa_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0 — Olive report"
subtitle: "ribostall global_occupancy — occupancy stats"
date: 2026-06-06
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [7, 23, 16, 16, 20, 18]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![2x3 grid of day-5-vs-day-0 amino-acid Fisher volcano panels: rows are conditions (top = BWM, bottom = control) and columns are ribosome sites (A-site / P-site / E-site, left to right). Each panel plots -log10(FDR) on the y-axis against log2(odds ratio) on the x-axis (x range about -0.6 to +0.6); each glyph is an amino-acid one-letter code coloured by physicochemical class (acidic = red, basic = blue, hydrophobic = green, neutral = orange, polar = purple). Triangles mark FDR<0.05 cells and circles mark non-significant cells; the dotted vertical line is log2(OR) = 0, the dashed vertical lines mark +/-0.5 log2(OR), and the dashed horizontal line marks FDR = 0.05.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/composite/aa_fisher_composite.png){fig-pos="H" width=92%}

Each glyph is a single amino acid at one (condition, site) BH-FDR family of 20. Positive log2(OR) means day-5-enriched relative to day-0 within the same condition; negative means day-5-depleted. The test does not compare BWM to control directly — that contrast is read by comparing the paired top (BWM) and bottom (control) rows of the grid panel by panel.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `aa_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `timepoint_fisher_within_condition`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `codon_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0.csv`
  - `aa_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0.csv`
  - `codon_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0.csv`
  - `aa_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5.csv`
  - `codon_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5.csv`
- **Test:** Fisher's exact (two-sided), within-condition day-5-vs-day-0 contrast per (condition, site, aa); BH-FDR within each (condition, site) family of 20 AAs
- **Design:** Per (condition, site, amino acid) 2x2 day-5-vs-day-0 contingency tested by two-sided Fisher's exact within one condition; the 2 biological replicates per (condition, timepoint) are summed before the test (pseudoreplicated); BH-FDR applied independently within each of 6 (condition, site) families of 20 amino acids (120 tests total). The test is within-condition: BWM and control are tested separately and never share a contingency. Effect column is `log2_OR` = log2 of the odds ratio (positive = day-5-enriched relative to day-0, negative = depleted).

### Family-level caveats

- **pseudorep** — 2 biological reps per (condition, timepoint) are summed into the 2x2 before Fisher's exact; the source script literally prints a pseudoreplication warning. Anti-conservative p-values.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative** — pooled totals are whole-transcriptome footprint counts (1e5-1e6); Fisher's exact returns vanishing or exactly-zero p-values for tiny absolute deviations. The odds ratio is the primary effect column, not p.
- **bh-per-(condition,site)** — BH-FDR is per (condition, site) within each contrast file (~20 aa or ~61 codon tests per family); `p_adj` means "corrected within this slice", not across the whole CSV.
- **within-condition-clean** — unlike the between-condition and between-timepoint Wilcoxon families, these tests do not mix conditions or timepoints; each Fisher is held within one (condition, contrast) cell. Structurally cleaner on the pooling-as-confound axis; a positive design feature.

---

## Headline

At AA-level within-condition Fisher d5 vs d0, 115/120 tests clear FDR<0.05 (BWM 56/60, control 59/60) — the most of any file in the family: 55 site x amino-acid cells are significant in both conditions, 1 in BWM only, 4 in control only. Minimum adjusted p underflows to 0.0 (13 cells) at whole-transcriptome pooled N (totals 2.0M-3.4M), so p magnitude is uninformative here and the informative axis is `effect change` (BWM `log2_OR` - control `log2_OR`); effects are small.

This contrast is shared-direction-dominant — BWM and control move the same way on most of the large cells, more so than either day-10 contrast. Both conditions deplete G at every site (A -0.253 / -0.226, P -0.377 / -0.229, E -0.204 / -0.112), deplete W at site A (-0.538 / -0.255) and P at site E (-0.327 / -0.340), and enrich N (A +0.251 / +0.227), M (A +0.186 / +0.230), D (A +0.273 / +0.074) and I (A +0.200 / +0.184). Reported at equal billing, the largest BWM-vs-control divergences are smaller and scattered: E:Y (BWM +0.099 vs control -0.131), A:C (BWM -0.113 vs control +0.095), E:K (BWM -0.080 vs control +0.101), P:M (BWM +0.154 vs control -0.041), E:H (BWM +0.132 vs control -0.049), E:T (BWM +0.070 vs control -0.111).

## Top hits

The effect column is `log2_OR` (log2 of the Fisher odds ratio for the day-5-vs-day-0 contrast within one condition; positive = day-5-enriched relative to day-0, negative = day-5-depleted). Each row pairs the BWM and control `log2_OR` for one (site, amino acid) cell. The `effect change` column is BWM `log2_OR` minus control `log2_OR`: near zero means both conditions moved together (a shared timecourse drift), large magnitude means the two conditions' day-5-vs-day-0 trajectories diverge — positive when BWM rose relative to control, negative when control rose relative to BWM. The three sections split every cell significant at FDR<0.05 in at least one condition by which condition(s) reach significance: both, BWM only, control only; cells significant in neither are omitted. Because the pooled totals are whole-transcriptome footprint counts almost every cell clears FDR<0.05 and p magnitude is uninformative, so significance is carried by section membership rather than a displayed p column, and ranking is by `|effect change|`, not by p. Within each section rows are grouped by `site` in A / P / E order, then sorted by `effect change` descending (no row cap). Each amino-acid one-letter code is expanded to `<letter> (<full name>)` on first appearance in each section.

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### Significant in both conditions

| site | amino acid | BWM (`log2_OR`) | control (`log2_OR`) | effect change | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | P (proline) | -0.154 | -0.379 | +0.225 |  |
| A | D (aspartate) | +0.273 | +0.074 | +0.199 |  |
| A | A (alanine) | -0.089 | -0.244 | +0.155 |  |
| A | V (valine) | +0.090 | -0.059 | +0.149 |  |
| A | F (phenylalanine) | +0.098 | +0.052 | +0.045 |  |
| A | T (threonine) | +0.070 | +0.035 | +0.034 |  |
| A | H (histidine) | -0.026 | -0.052 | +0.026 |  |
| A | N (asparagine) | +0.251 | +0.227 | +0.024 |  |
| A | L (leucine) | -0.099 | -0.118 | +0.018 |  |
| A | I (isoleucine) | +0.200 | +0.184 | +0.016 |  |
| A | Q (glutamine) | -0.025 | -0.031 | +0.006 |  |
| A | R (arginine) | -0.021 | -0.024 | +0.003 |  |
| A | S (serine) | +0.023 | +0.048 | -0.025 |  |
| A | G (glycine) | -0.253 | -0.226 | -0.028 |  |
| A | M (methionine) | +0.186 | +0.230 | -0.044 |  |
| A | K (lysine) | -0.045 | +0.114 | -0.159 |  |
| A | C (cysteine) | -0.113 | +0.095 | -0.208 |  |
| A | W (tryptophan) | -0.538 | -0.255 | -0.284 |  |
| P | H | +0.084 | -0.112 | +0.196 |  |
| P | M | +0.154 | -0.041 | +0.195 |  |
| P | K | -0.021 | -0.201 | +0.181 |  |
| P | P | -0.072 | -0.243 | +0.171 |  |
| P | L | +0.170 | +0.059 | +0.111 |  |
| P | Y (tyrosine) | +0.069 | -0.031 | +0.100 |  |
| P | T | +0.060 | -0.031 | +0.091 |  |
| P | R | -0.048 | -0.091 | +0.043 |  |
| P | E (glutamate) | +0.058 | +0.058 | +0.001 |  |
| P | S | +0.094 | +0.102 | -0.008 |  |
| P | C | +0.072 | +0.115 | -0.043 |  |
| P | A | -0.088 | -0.043 | -0.045 |  |
| P | I | +0.070 | +0.121 | -0.051 |  |
| P | N | +0.082 | +0.137 | -0.055 |  |
| P | G | -0.377 | -0.229 | -0.149 |  |
| P | V | -0.015 | +0.134 | -0.149 |  |
| P | F | +0.037 | +0.210 | -0.173 |  |
| P | D | -0.061 | +0.154 | -0.216 |  |
| E | Y | +0.099 | -0.131 | +0.230 |  |
| E | H | +0.132 | -0.049 | +0.181 |  |
| E | T | +0.070 | -0.111 | +0.180 |  |
| E | A | -0.052 | -0.199 | +0.147 |  |
| E | F | +0.062 | -0.071 | +0.133 |  |
| E | S | +0.198 | +0.082 | +0.117 |  |
| E | V | -0.024 | -0.064 | +0.039 |  |
| E | P | -0.327 | -0.340 | +0.013 |  |
| E | L | +0.069 | +0.057 | +0.012 |  |
| E | Q | +0.065 | +0.058 | +0.007 |  |
| E | D | +0.035 | +0.056 | -0.022 |  |
| E | C | +0.064 | +0.094 | -0.030 |  |
| E | E | +0.057 | +0.106 | -0.049 |  |
| E | N | +0.059 | +0.118 | -0.059 |  |
| E | G | -0.204 | -0.112 | -0.092 |  |
| E | R | -0.051 | +0.080 | -0.130 |  |
| E | M | +0.083 | +0.235 | -0.152 |  |
| E | K | -0.080 | +0.101 | -0.181 |  |
| E | W | -0.058 | +0.151 | -0.209 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### Significant in BWM only

| site | amino acid | BWM (`log2_OR`) | control (`log2_OR`) | effect change | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | Y (tyrosine) | -0.151 | -0.002 | -0.149 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### Significant in control only

| site | amino acid | BWM (`log2_OR`) | control (`log2_OR`) | effect change | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | E (glutamate) | +0.007 | +0.139 | -0.131 |  |
| P | W (tryptophan) | +0.013 | -0.207 | +0.219 |  |
| P | Q (glutamine) | +0.010 | -0.175 | +0.185 |  |
| E | I (isoleucine) | +0.005 | -0.057 | +0.062 |  |

### Flag glossary

No rows are flagged at amino-acid resolution: every (condition, site, amino acid) cell has both day-5 and day-0 counts well above the test's stability threshold (the `within_condition_sig_split` low-count cutoffs of BWM < 100 / control < 200), so the `flag` column is empty across all three sections. The `low-count` flag that the codon sister CSV carries (where the in-frame stop TGA trips it) does not fire here.

## Numbers at a glance
- `n_tests`: 120 (60 per condition; 3 sites x 20 AAs each)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 115 (BWM 56/60, control 59/60)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 115 (no cell sits in the [0.05, 0.10) band)
- `min adjusted-p`: 0.0 (underflow; 13 cells at exactly 0.0). Smallest non-zero p_adj is 1.986e-296 (A, control, I).
- `p_floor`: n/a — Fisher with pooled N in the millions has no meaningful floor; the dominant concern is `large-N-Fisher-anticonservative` (family-wide).
- Per (condition, site) sig at FDR<0.05: BWM A 19/20, P 18/20, E 19/20; control A 19/20, P 20/20, E 20/20.
- Section split: 55 cells sig in both, 1 BWM-only, 4 control-only (60 of 60 site x aa cells sig in >=1 condition).

## Biological interpretation

**At whole-transcriptome read depth almost every cell is statistically significant, so effect size, not p-value, is the axis that carries information (concrete).** 115 of 120 (condition, site, amino acid) tests clear FDR<0.05 — the highest count of any file in the family — and 13 cells underflow to an adjusted p of exactly 0.0. With pooled day-5 + day-0 footprint totals in the millions (2.0M-3.4M per arm), Fisher's exact returns vanishing p-values for even tiny relative shifts, so "significant in both / BWM only / control only" is a weak way to rank cells. The quantity that does carry information is `effect change` — how far apart the two conditions' day-5-vs-day-0 trajectories sit — and those magnitudes are small (the largest is A:W at -0.284, then E:Y at +0.230).

**This is the most coordinated contrast in the family: both conditions move the same way on nearly all the large cells (concrete).** Unlike the two day-10 contrasts, where the largest cells were genuine BWM-vs-control direction splits, the d5-vs-d0 step is shared-direction-dominant. Glycine is depleted at all three sites in both arms (A -0.253 / -0.226, P -0.377 / -0.229, E -0.204 / -0.112), tryptophan is depleted at the A site in both (-0.538 / -0.255), proline is depleted at the E site in both (-0.327 / -0.340), and asparagine, methionine, aspartate and isoleucine are all enriched at the A site in both (N +0.251 / +0.227, M +0.186 / +0.230, D +0.273 / +0.074, I +0.200 / +0.184). Because each arm is tested only against its own day-0 baseline, co-directional cells are the least-confounded reading of a within-condition contrast: both lines move their occupancy the same way from day 0 to day 5, so the BWM perturbation is unlikely to be what drives them. Read together, these cells describe a shared early-timecourse drift — most prominently a coordinated loss of glycine and tryptophan occupancy and a coordinated gain of the A-site N/D/M/I set — that is common to perturbed and unperturbed lines.

**The genuine BWM-vs-control divergences are present but small and scattered, the muted version of the day-10 design-target signal (concrete).** The largest direction-split cells are E:Y (BWM +0.099 vs control -0.131), A:C (BWM -0.113 vs control +0.095), E:K (BWM -0.080 vs control +0.101), P:M (BWM +0.154 vs control -0.041), E:H (BWM +0.132 vs control -0.049) and E:T (BWM +0.070 vs control -0.111). These are the cells where the two lines' day-5-vs-day-0 trajectories actually part ways, and they are the design-target reading of a within-condition contrast — but their `effect change` magnitudes (the largest is E:Y at +0.230) are well below the divergences seen at day 10, and several cluster at the E site rather than the A site. The single BWM-only cell (A:Y, BWM depleted while control is flat) and the four control-only cells (A:E, P:W, P:Q enriched in control only; E:I) are likewise low-magnitude, consistent with section membership being barely discriminating in this file. The basic and charged residues that anchor exit-tunnel and decoding biology (E:K, E:H at the E site; A:C at the A site) are where the small BWM-specific signal concentrates, but at this contrast it is a directional hint, not a strong effect.

**Whether the shared depletions and the small divergences are amino-acid-level or driven by single synonymous codons is the open question for the codon sister (exploratory).** The shared glycine and tryptophan depletions, the A-site N/D/M/I enrichments, and the scattered E-site splits are reported here at amino-acid resolution, which aggregates each residue's synonymous codons. A shared depletion or a divergence that is genuine at the residue level should persist when the same contrast is read at codon resolution; one that collapses onto a single synonym points instead to a codon-usage signature. Glycine is the natural cell to take first — it has the most flexible backbone of the 20 residues and decodes through four synonymous codons, so its coordinated depletion at all three sites could be carried by one synonym or spread across all four. This is a hypothesis to test against `codon_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0`, not a finding from this file — directional, not established here.

**Whether the d5-vs-d0 picture is the first leg of a consistent trajectory is a follow-up check, not a conclusion (exploratory).** This file compares day 5 to day 0, the d0-anchored first step of the timecourse. Reading it alongside `aa_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5` and `aa_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0` would show whether the A-site trajectory is additive (does d10_vs_d0 approximately equal d5_vs_d0 + d10_vs_d5 per cell?) or biphasic — the d10_vs_d5 sister already flags that its A:W and A:P control enrichments run opposite to the d10_vs_d0 picture, so the d5-vs-d0 leg is the missing piece of that decomposition. Separately, the shared-direction cells (the glycine depletions, the A-site N/D/M/I enrichments) should be checked against `within_condition_binomial`: a cell that is a stable baseline across all six condition-by-timepoint groups is a standing compositional feature both lines carry, not a day-5-vs-day-0 effect. Both are cross-checks to carry forward, not claims this file establishes.

**What to carry forward.** *Concrete*: the near-universal significance at whole-transcriptome N (read by `effect change`, not p); the shared-direction depletion of glycine at all three sites and tryptophan at the A site, plus the shared A-site enrichment of N/D/M/I, as condition-independent early-timecourse drift; and the muted, scattered BWM-vs-control divergences (E:Y, A:C, E:K) as the design-clean but weak divergence signal in this contrast. *Exploratory*: whether the shared glycine depletion and the small E-site splits localise to single synonymous codons (the codon sister), and whether the A-site trajectory is additive or biphasic across the d10_vs_d5 and d10_vs_d0 contrasts. *Noise*: none flagged at amino-acid resolution — every cell's counts are well above the stability threshold, so no row here is discounted for low support (the codon sister's in-frame stop TGA is the first place that changes).

## Methods
Fisher's exact (two-sided) is applied to a 2x2 of (aa_count, total - aa_count) at day_5 vs day_0 within each condition. `global_codon_occ_stats.py` (Analysis 3b) pools the replicate counts per (condition, timepoint) before the test and applies BH-FDR within each (condition, site) family of 20 AAs; each of the 6 (condition, site) sub-families is corrected independently, not across the full 120-row file. The effect is `log2_OR` (>0 = enriched at day_5 relative to day_0, <0 = depleted). The test does **not** compare BWM against control directly (that is the `control-vs-BWM-divergent-direction` reading) and does not test enrichment vs the transcriptomic background (the `within_condition_binomial` family).

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **pseudorep.** The 2 biological replicates per (condition, timepoint) are summed into the 2x2 before Fisher, so per-replicate variance is not in the test statistic — read `log2_OR` as the primary effect column and treat the smallest p-magnitudes as inflated, not as extra certainty.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative.** Pooled totals are whole-transcriptome footprint counts (day_5 ~2.01M BWM / ~2.41M control; day_0 ~2.11M BWM / ~3.37M control), so even tiny relative deviations drive p toward 0 (13 cells underflow to exactly 0.0, the most in the family) — rank by `log2_OR` and `effect change`, never by p.
- **bh-per-(condition,site).** BH is applied within each of the 6 (condition, site) families of 20 AAs, so `p_adj` means "corrected within this sub-family" — compare cells within a (condition, site) slice, not across the 120-row grid.
- **within-condition-clean.** No condition pooling and no timepoint pooling cross-contaminate the test; the file cleanly answers "does day_5 differ from day_0 within this condition", leaving the BWM-vs-control comparison to be read by pairing the two arms (the divergent-direction cells below).
- **control-vs-BWM-divergent-direction.** This contrast is shared-direction-dominant, so divergences are fewer and smaller than in d10_vs_d0 / d10_vs_d5: E:Y (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.099 / control -0.131), A:C (-0.113 / +0.095), E:K (-0.080 / +0.101), P:M (+0.154 / -0.041), E:H (+0.132 / -0.049), E:T (+0.070 / -0.111) — these are the design-target reading and should anchor the BWM-specific reading at the synthesis layer, but with modest weight given their magnitude.
- **near-universal-sig-large-N.** 115/120 tests clear FDR<0.05 (the highest in the family), so "both / BWM-only / control-only" section membership is weakly discriminating — rank by `|effect change|`, treating section membership as context rather than as the effect.

**Considered and ruled out**

- **rare-aa-low-count.** Every (condition, site, aa) cell has both day_5 and day_0 counts well above the `within_condition_sig_split` thresholds (BWM < 100 / control < 200), so no `low-count` flag fires at AA resolution — ruled out here (the codon sister differs, where in-frame stop TGA trips it).

---

## Plots — individual

![Day-5-vs-day-0 BWM A-site amino-acid volcano: log2(OR) on x (range +/-0.6), -log10(FDR) on y; triangles are FDR<0.05, glyphs coloured by amino-acid class. Tryptophan (W, hydrophobic green) ~= -0.54 is the far-left depletion and the panel's largest-magnitude cell, with glycine (G, neutral orange) ~= -0.25 and proline (P, orange) ~= -0.15 also depleted at the top; aspartate (D, acidic red) ~= +0.27, asparagine (N, polar purple) ~= +0.25 and isoleucine (I, hydrophobic green) ~= +0.20 are the far-right enrichments. Cysteine (C, polar purple) ~= -0.11 and threonine (T, purple) ~= +0.07 sit mid-panel; lysine (K, basic blue) ~= -0.05 lower; glutamate (E, acidic red) ~= +0.01 sits at the origin as the one non-significant cell (a circle).](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/individual/BWM_A_volcano.png)

![Day-5-vs-day-0 BWM P-site amino-acid volcano: glycine (G, neutral orange) ~= -0.38 is the far-left depletion and the panel's largest-magnitude cell, sitting alone at top-left; leucine (L, hydrophobic green) ~= +0.17, methionine (M, green) ~= +0.15 and serine (S, polar purple) ~= +0.09 are the rightmost enrichments at the top, and alanine (A, green) ~= -0.09 is depleted near them. Aspartate (D, acidic red) ~= -0.06 and proline (P, orange) ~= -0.07 sit one tier down; most remaining residues cluster near the origin within +/-0.1 log2(OR). The two control-only cells (W, Q) are absent from this BWM panel.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/individual/BWM_P_volcano.png)

![Day-5-vs-day-0 BWM E-site amino-acid volcano: proline (P, neutral orange) ~= -0.33 and glycine (G, orange) ~= -0.20 are the far-left depletions at the top, with lysine (K, basic blue) ~= -0.08 also depleted; serine (S, polar purple) ~= +0.20 is the rightmost enrichment and histidine (H, basic blue) ~= +0.13 sits below it. Tyrosine (Y, hydrophobic green) ~= +0.10, methionine (M, green) ~= +0.08 and leucine (L, green) ~= +0.07 are mid-panel enrichments; tryptophan (W, green) ~= -0.06 and arginine (R, blue) ~= -0.05 are the small depletions; most cells fall within +/-0.2 log2(OR).](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/individual/BWM_E_volcano.png)

![Day-5-vs-day-0 control A-site amino-acid volcano: the most spread of the six panels. Proline (P, neutral orange) ~= -0.38, tryptophan (W, hydrophobic green) ~= -0.26, alanine (A, green) ~= -0.24 and glycine (G, orange) ~= -0.23 are the far-left depletions at the top; methionine (M, hydrophobic green) ~= +0.23, asparagine (N, polar purple) ~= +0.23 and isoleucine (I, green) ~= +0.18 are the far-right enrichments, with lysine (K, basic blue) ~= +0.11 and aspartate (D, acidic red) ~= +0.07 between them. Valine (V, green) ~= -0.06 sits mid-panel; cysteine (C, purple) ~= +0.10 and serine (S, purple) ~= +0.05 lower; tyrosine (Y, green) ~= +0.02 is the one non-significant cell (a circle at the origin).](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/individual/control_A_volcano.png)

![Day-5-vs-day-0 control P-site amino-acid volcano (all 20 cells significant): phenylalanine (F, hydrophobic green) ~= +0.21, aspartate (D, acidic red) ~= +0.15, asparagine (N, polar purple) ~= +0.14, valine (V, green) ~= +0.13 and isoleucine (I, green) ~= +0.12 are the rightmost enrichments at the top; glycine (G, neutral orange) ~= -0.23, proline (P, orange) ~= -0.24, tryptophan (W, green) ~= -0.21 and lysine (K, basic blue) ~= -0.20 are the leftmost depletions. Cysteine (C, polar purple) ~= +0.12, leucine (L, green) ~= +0.06 and glutamate (E, acidic red) ~= +0.06 sit mid-panel; methionine (M, green) ~= -0.04 and tyrosine (Y, green) ~= -0.03 are the smallest depletions near the FDR line.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/individual/control_P_volcano.png)

![Day-5-vs-day-0 control E-site amino-acid volcano (all 20 cells significant): methionine (M, hydrophobic green) ~= +0.24 is the far-right enrichment and proline (P, neutral orange) ~= -0.34 is the far-left depletion, both at the top; alanine (A, green) ~= -0.20, tyrosine (Y, green) ~= -0.13 and threonine (T, polar purple) ~= -0.11 are the other top-left depletions, with asparagine (N, purple) ~= +0.12, lysine (K, basic blue) ~= +0.10 and serine (S, purple) ~= +0.08 enriched near the top. Tryptophan (W, green) ~= +0.15 and leucine (L, green) ~= +0.06 sit mid-panel; glutamate (E, red) ~= +0.11, glutamine (Q, purple) ~= +0.06 and cysteine (C, purple) ~= +0.09 lower; histidine (H, blue) ~= -0.05 sits near the FDR line.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/individual/control_E_volcano.png)
